creator: raimeetravel
caption:
A totally different side of Japan: here’s everywhere we stopped 👇 

📍 Ojiragawa Valley
📍 Fumotto Base Camp
📍 Lumière Winery
📍 Roppongi
📍 Candeo Hotels Tokyo Roppongi

Comment “PATH” and I’ll DM you my full blog post breaking down each stop + how to do this in one day.

#Japan #Tokyo #tokyotokyo #2025connectedjapan @tokyotokyooldmeetsnew

transcription.text: If you want to escape the tourists in Japan, here's where you're going to go. And this itinerary will show you mountains, wineries, and calm nature, all not too far from Tokyo. You'll find all of this in the Yamanashi Prefecture. You can start your day by exploring this valley. The water here is insane emerald color straight from the Southern Alps and the whole place feels untouched. We were the only people here. Then you have to go check out Fumoto. It's an outdoor base camp style spot at the foot of the mountains with local food, little shops, and the prettiest open air spaces. We had one of the best lunches I've ever had in Japan. It was so fresh. Then you can go tour Lumiere Winery which has been making wine for almost 140 years and they still have Japan's first European style stone fermentation cellar. I didn't even know Japan had wine and this felt like stepping back in time. Then you can spend a few more days exploring this prefecture. Check out my other videos for itineraries for each of those days. This was part four of getting off the beaten path in Japan and this Yamanashi route is such a perfect add-on to any Tokyo trip.
